challenges.title: Your First CSS Rule
challenges.description: Write a CSS rule that targets all `p` elements and sets their `color` to `blue` and their `font-size` to `16px`. Assign your CSS to the variable `css`.
challenges.test_code:
assert 'p' in css, 'Should target p elements'
assert 'color' in css.lower(), 'Should set the color property'
assert 'blue' in css.lower(), 'Should set color to blue'
assert 'font-size' in css.lower(), 'Should set the font-size property'
assert '16px' in css.lower(), 'Should set font-size to 16px'
assert '{' in css and '}' in css, 'Should have curly braces for the rule block'
hints: A CSS rule has the form: selector { property: value; }
Use `color: blue;` to set text color and `font-size: 16px;` to set size

challenges.title: Element, Class, and ID Selectors
challenges.description: Write three CSS rules: (1) target all `h1` elements and set `color` to `red`, (2) target the class `.highlight` and set `background-color` to `yellow`, (3) target the ID `#main` and set `font-size` to `20px`. Assign your CSS to the variable `css`.
challenges.test_code:
assert 'h1' in css.lower(), 'Should target h1 elements'
assert '.highlight' in css, 'Should target the .highlight class'
assert '#main' in css, 'Should target the #main ID'
assert 'color' in css.lower(), 'Should use the color property'
assert 'red' in css.lower(), 'Should set color to red'
assert 'background-color' in css.lower(), 'Should use background-color property'
assert 'yellow' in css.lower(), 'Should set background-color to yellow'
assert 'font-size' in css.lower(), 'Should use font-size property'
assert '20px' in css.lower(), 'Should set font-size to 20px'
hints: Class selectors start with a dot: .classname { }
ID selectors start with a hash: #idname { }
Element selectors use the tag name directly: h1 { }
